text: What if I told you that plants have a hidden power? This is the Calvin cycle, a crucial process in photosynthesis. It transforms carbon dioxide into energy. First up, carbon fixation. Carbon dioxide combines with a five-carbon molecule. Sounds simple, right? Then, energy molecules from the light reactions help convert that into sugars. This is where plants make their food! But here’s the twist: this process can be influenced by temperature and light. It’s not always perfect. Want to dive deeper into the secrets of plants? Hit that subscribe button for more science!
